items.title: What are the main events for today?
items.summary: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than the ECB accounts. This is never a market moving report as the information is outdated by the time it's released.  At the moment, the markets are just extending the pullbacks from yesterday's lows after Trump started to de-escalate the situation with Iran. After that, we are probably going to consolidate again awaiting the US CPI report on Tuesday.  AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the only highlight is the US Jobless Claims data. Initial Claims are expected at 217K vs 215K prior, while Continuing Claims are seen at 1814K vs 1814K prior. This shouldn't be a market moving release unless we see a very big deviation from the expected numbers. The US labour market has been strengthening since the start of the year, with the unemployment rate falling to 4.2% in June from a peak of 4.5% seen in November 2025. The Fed has shifted its focus back to inflation as that's the part of the mandate more in tension now.  CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - Fed's Williams (neutral - voter) 16:00 GMT/12:00 ET - SNB's Martin (neutral - voter) 17:30 GMT/13:30 ET - Fed's Logan (hawkish - voter) 19:30 GMT/15:30 ET - BoE's Breeden (neutral - voter) This article was written by flfeaa2662d774455a8d50fa77b791ed5f at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲时段 在欧洲时段，除了欧洲央行财务报表外，议程上没有太多值得关注的内容。这份报告通常不会对市场产生影响，因为其发布时数据早已过时。目前，随着特朗普开始缓和与伊朗的紧张局势，市场正延续昨日低点以来的回调走势。此后，市场可能将再次进入盘整状态，静待周二公布的美国CPI报告。  美国时段 在美国时段，唯一的焦点是美国初请失业金人数数据。初请失业金人数预计为21.7万，前值为21.5万；续请失业金人数预计为181.4万，前值同样为181.4万。除非实际数据与预期出现巨大偏差，否则该数据发布不太可能引发市场波动。自年初以来，美国劳动力市场持续走强，失业率已从2025年11月4.5%的高点降至6月的4.2%。 美联储已将关注重点重新转向通胀，因为这是其职责中目前面临更大压力的部分。  央行官员讲话 格林尼治标准时间13:00/美国东部时间09:00 - 美联储威廉姆斯（中性——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间16:00/美国东部时间12:00 - 瑞士央行马丁（中性——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间17:30/美国东部时间13:30 - 美联储洛根（鹰派——有投票权） 格林尼治标准时间19:30/美国东部时间15:30 - 英国央行布里登（中性——有投票权） 本文由 investinglive.com 网站的 flfeaa2662d774455a8d50fa77b791ed5f 撰写。

After that, we are probably going to consolidate again awaiting the US CPI report on Tuesday.  AMERICAN SESSION In the American session, the only highlight is the US Jobless Claims data. The US labour market has been strengthening since the start of the year, with the unemployment rate falling to 4.2% in June from a peak of 4.5% seen in November 2025. The Fed has shifted its focus back to inflation as that's the part of the mandate more in tension now.  CENTRAL BANK SPEAKERS 13:00 GMT/09:00 ET - Fed's Williams (neutral - voter) 16:00 GMT/12:00 ET - SNB's Martin (neutral - voter) 17:30 GMT/13:30 ET - Fed's Logan (hawkish - voter) 19:30 GMT/15:30 ET - BoE's Breeden (neutral - voter) This article was written by flfeaa2662d774455a8d50fa77b791ed5f at investinglive.com.

items.title: Japan's Katayama vows fiscal trust as ruling party tweaks economic blueprint
items.summary: Katayama's refusal to comment on specific bond yield levels keeps the government's usual distance from JGB market moves, leaving traders to price yields off underlying fundamentals rather than any fresh verbal steer from the finance ministry. Her emphasis on securing market trust in fiscal sustainability signals continuity rather than a shift in stance, and should limit any near-term repricing of long-end yields. The acknowledgement that the ruling party is adjusting wording in the economic blueprint points to possible fiscal changes ahead, a detail bond and FX desks will watch closely. Her comment that the BOJ can adjust policy regardless of government messaging reinforces the central bank's independence and keeps rate-path expectations anchored to the BOJ's own signals rather than political pressure. --- Katayama is playing it straight down the line, deferring on yields and monetary policy while leaning on fiscal credibility to keep markets onside. Summary: Katayama said she won't comment on specific bond yield levels She is aware the ruling party is adjusting wording in the economic blueprint She reiterated that specific monetary policy tools are up to the BOJ She said it is important that the government's stance has been ensured to have market trust She said she is closely monitoring economic indicators and market situations She said the government will ensure fiscal sustainability to gain market trust She said the BOJ can adjust monetary policy regardless of what the government says Japan's Finance Minister Katayama said on Thursday she would not comment on specific bond yield levels, sticking to the government's long-standing practice of avoiding direct market commentary even as fiscal and monetary policy questions draw growing attention from investors. Katayama said she is aware that the ruling party is adjusting the wording of the government's economic blueprint, a process already flagged separately by Economy Minister Kiuchi, suggesting fiscal settings could shift somewhat as coalition lawmakers weigh in on the draft. She was careful to separate that fiscal work from monetary policy, repeating that specific monetary tools remain entirely up to the Bank of Japan and adding that the central bank can adjust policy regardless of what the government says. She placed heavy emphasis on market trust, saying it is important that the government's fiscal stance has been ensured to carry credibility with investors, and pledged that the government will work to secure fiscal sustainability in order to maintain that trust. Katayama said she is closely monitoring both economic indicators and market conditions, language consistent with previous guidance suggesting Tokyo remains attentive to bond market dynamics without committing to specific intervention thresholds. Her remarks arrive alongside comments from Economy Minister Kiuchi earlier the same evening, who similarly stressed that the government would not pre-signal any preference to the BOJ on the timing or direction of a policy shift. Read together, the two sets of comments reinforce a consistent government message that fiscal and monetary responsibilities are kept firmly separate, with Tokyo focused on shoring up its own fiscal credibility while leaving interest rate and yield decisions to the BOJ and the market respectively. The blueprint revision process is likely to remain a focal point for investors in coming weeks as further details on wording and fiscal commitments emerge. This article was written by fl6553e4b45d84486a91658a8b3f02bf22 at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 片山拒绝就具体债券收益率水平置评，这体现了政府一贯对日本国债市场波动保持的距离，使得交易员只能根据基本面因素而非财务省的最新口头指引来定价收益率。她强调要赢得市场对财政可持续性的信任，这表明政策立场保持连续性而非发生转变，应能限制长期债券收益率在短期内的重新定价。执政党承认正在调整经济蓝图的措辞，这预示着未来可能出现财政政策调整，债券和外汇交易部门将密切关注这一细节。她表示日本央行可以不受政府表态影响而调整政策，这强化了央行的独立性，并使利率路径预期继续锚定于日本央行自身的信号，而非政治压力。--- 片山采取了一贯的稳健路线，在收益率和货币政策上保持克制，同时依靠财政公信力来维系市场信心。 摘要：片山表示，她不会就具体的债券收益率水平发表评论。她了解到执政党正在调整经济蓝图中的措辞。她重申，具体的货币政策工具由日本央行决定。她表示，重要的是政府 立场已获得市场信任这一点至关重要。她表示正在密切关注经济指标和市场状况。她表示，政府将确保财政可持续性以赢得市场信任。她表示，无论政府如何表态，日本央行均可调整货币政策。日本财务大臣片山周四表示，她不会对具体的债券收益率水平发表评论，坚持政府长期以来的做法——避免直接评论市场，尽管财政和货币政策问题正日益引起投资者的关注。片山表示，她了解到执政党正在调整政府经济蓝图的措辞——经济大臣木内此前已单独提及这一过程——这表明随着执政联盟议员对草案发表意见，财政政策方向可能会有所调整。 她谨慎地将财政工作与货币政策区分开来，重申具体的货币政策工具完全由日本央行决定，并补充说，无论政府如何表态，央行都可以调整政策。她特别强调了市场信任的重要性，指出确保政府的财政立场在投资者中具有公信力至关重要，并承诺政府将致力于保障财政可持续性，以维持这种信任。片山表示，她正在密切关注经济指标和市场状况，这一表述与此前发布的指引一致，表明东京方面仍在关注债券市场的动态，但并未承诺具体的干预门槛。她的这番言论与当晚早些时候经济大臣木内发表的评论相呼应，后者同样强调，政府不会就政策转变的时机或方向向日本央行发出任何预先信号。 综合来看，这两组评论强化了政府的一贯立场，即财政与货币职责严格分离：东京方面专注于巩固自身的财政公信力，而利率和收益率的决策则分别由日本央行和市场决定。随着措辞和财政承诺的进一步细节浮出水面，蓝图修订进程在未来几周内仍将是投资者关注的焦点。本文由 investinglive.com 网站的 fl6553e4b45d84486a91658a8b3f02bf22 撰写。

items.title: Japan chief cabinet secretary says government is watching markets with high sense of urgency
items.summary: Government is watching markets with very high sense of urgency Long-term interest rates are decided by markets based on various factors Want to secure market trust by stably lowering government debt-to-GDP ratio It's not direct verbal intervention and jawboning towards the Japanese yen currency situation, but the headline remark is indirectly alluding to that. The Japanese government continues to reaffirm that Takaichi's fiscal policy approach remains the right way to go. However, that hasn't quite been reflected by the economic situation and market confidence since last year already. The US-Iran war definitely doesn't help in that regard and more so with the BOJ also raising interest rates at a time when Japan's fiscal position and debt risks are mounting. USD/JPY is down 0.1% to 162.35 on the day but continues to sit higher on the week, following last Thursday's sharp drop. As much as the dollar might be struggling, the yen's plight looks to be even worse. As such, the path of least resistance remains for a move higher in USD/JPY. The only key risk to be wary about is intervention fears, with Japan's ministry of finance watching over things rather closely now. That especially with the currency pair trading around its highest level in 40 years now. This article was written by fl9bde53b91e184082bbe3aa3acaaf2cb0 at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 政府正以极强的紧迫感密切关注市场动向。长期利率由市场根据各种因素决定。希望通过稳定降低政府债务占GDP的比例来赢得市场信任。这并非针对日元汇率的直接口头干预或言语施压，但这一表态间接暗示了这一点。日本政府继续重申，高市洋子（Takaichi）的财政政策方针仍是正确的道路。然而，自去年以来，经济形势和市场信心尚未充分反映出这一点。美伊冲突无疑加剧了这一局面，更何况在日本财政状况和债务风险日益加剧之际，日本央行也上调了利率。美元兑日元当日下跌0.1%至162.35，但在上周四大幅下跌后，本周仍维持在较高水平。尽管美元可能面临困境，但日元的处境似乎更为严峻。因此，美元兑日元走高的阻力最小。唯一需要警惕的关键风险是市场对日本政府干预的担忧，目前日本财务省正密切关注事态发展。 尤其是该货币对目前正徘徊在40年来的最高水平附近。本文由 fl9bde53b91e184082bbe3aa3acaaf2cb0 撰写，发表于 investinglive.com。

The Japanese government continues to reaffirm that Takaichi's fiscal policy approach remains the right way to go. However, that hasn't quite been reflected by the economic situation and market confidence since last year already. The US-Iran war definitely doesn't help in that regard and more so with the BOJ also raising interest rates at a time when Japan's fiscal position and debt risks are mounting. USD/JPY is down 0.1% to 162.35 on the day but continues to sit higher on the week, following last Thursday's sharp drop. As much as the dollar might be struggling, the yen's plight looks to be even worse. As such, the path of least resistance remains for a move higher in USD/JPY. The only key risk to be wary about is intervention fears, with Japan's ministry of finance watching over things rather closely now. That especially with the currency pair trading around its highest level in 40 years now. This article was written by fl9bde53b91e184082bbe3aa3acaaf2cb0 at investinglive.com.
